title: Sutera atropurpurea
taxonomy: Sutera atropurpurea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as figworts. This family is part of the larger order Lamiales, which includes plants such as mint, basil, and snapdragons. The Scrophulariaceae family is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world and is known for its wide range of flowering plants. Sutera atropurpurea, also known as African foxglove or atropurpurea suture, is an annual or perennial plant that is native to the tropical regions of Africa. It is often cultivated as an ornamental plant due to its attractive, tubular flowers in shades of purple.

The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in reducing stress, promoting relaxation, and improving respiratory health.
